title: Navigate to documentation and install an integration

blocks.content:
Integrations provide pre-built monitoring solutions for specific technologies, applications, and infrastructure components. They include dashboards, alerts, and data collection configurations that help you monitor systems without building everything from scratch.

Grafana Cloud offers integrations for popular technologies including Kubernetes, Docker, databases, cloud services, and applications. These integrations accelerate your observability setup by providing tested configurations and proven monitoring practices.
In this section, you'll open the integrations docs in a new tab so you can keep this guide and your Grafana stack open.
Open the **Integrations reference** documentation in a new browser tab.
Browse the available integrations to find one that matches your technology stack. For example, if you want to monitor a Linux server, look for **Linux server**.
Open the integration page and review the prerequisites, installation steps, and configuration options.
You opened the docs in a separate tab and reviewed the integration you plan to install.

blocks.title: Install an integration in Grafana Cloud

blocks.content: In this section, you'll open **Connections**, find an integration, and start the install flow. The steps use **Linux Server** as an example — choose the integration that matches your environment.
In the Grafana side menu, click **Connections > Add new connection**.
In the search bar, enter `Linux` (or the name of the integration you chose from the docs).
Click the **Linux Server** tile (or the tile for your chosen integration).
Review the integration overview, then follow the installation wizard: configure settings for your environment, install any required agents or collectors on your host, and verify data collection.
When the UI offers an **Install** control for dashboards and alerts, click **Install**.
Click **View Dashboards** to open the pre-built dashboards for the integration.
You located an integration and started the install flow. Pre-built dashboards and alerts give you immediate visibility into the technology you chose.

The integration is configured and actively monitoring your technology when data collection is working. The included alerts help identify potential issues.

In the next milestone, you'll configure billing and usage alerts to manage costs and prevent unexpected charges.

---

### More to explore (optional)

At this point in your journey, you can explore the following paths:

- [Grafana Cloud integrations catalog](https://grafana.com/docs/grafana-cloud/monitor-infrastructure/integrations/)
- [Monitor a Linux server in Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/linux-server-integration/)
